methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $7.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hugging Face
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

private.name: ElevenLabs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $3B+

private.name: Hive AI

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Wayve is a Series D artificial intelligence and software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall. The analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private-stage peers across the AI & Software sector, supplemented by one public comparable (PLTR), providing reasonable reference points for valuation triangulation.

Two methodologies were applied. The stage-progression method, anchored to Wayve's last post-money valuation of $8.6B, applies a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier for progression from Series D, yielding $12.9B. The peer-median method examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables—Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), Hive AI ($2.0B), and Labelbox ($1.0B)—which span a range of $1.0B to $7.0B with a median of $3.0B. This dispersion reflects material valuation variance within the peer set.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.5B (low) to $14.8B (high), with a median of $8.0B. Wayve's current $8.6B valuation aligns near the high end of peer medians and reflects its capital intensity and funding history ($2.8B raised to date). Key uncertainties include relative technology differentiation versus Cohere and Hugging Face, commercialization timelines, and near-term market conditions for AI software valuations, which remain subject to significant volatility.
